data.snippet: Suppliers to U.S. aerospace and semiconductor firms face worsening rare earth shortages, with two turning away some clients, industry insiders said, weeks before U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping for a summit in Beijing.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Rare earth shortages worsen in US aerospace, chips despite trade truce, sources say

data.text: Exclusive-Rare earth shortages worsen in US aerospace, chips despite trade truce, sources say

data.snippet: Europe's steel industry said on Thursday that EU provisions due to be set out next week that prioritise the use of locally-made materials must include steel, and 'local' should be understood as only close EU neighbours such as Britain and Norway.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Steel worker at furnace of ThyssenKrupp's steel plant in Duisburg

data.text: EU steel sector pushes to be part of 'Made in Europe' act, seeks narrow scope

data.snippet: Gold prices were little changed on Thursday as investors stayed on the sidelines ahead of the third round of indirect U.S.-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva, hoping for signals on whether the negotiations could ease geopolitical tensions.

image.text: Demand for gold bars rises people seek safe-haven investments amid market volatility in Egypt

data.text: Gold steady as markets await US-Iran nuclear talks outcome

data.snippet: Global aluminium producers have offered Japanese buyers premiums of $220 to $250 per metric ton for April to June shipments, up 13% to 28% from the current quarter and reflecting higher overseas premiums, three sources said on Thursday.

image.text: Aluminium bar stock is seen inside a factory in Dongguan

data.text: Global aluminium producers seek Q2 premiums of $220-$250/T from Japan buyers, sources say

data.snippet: China's lithium price surged on Thursday after Zimbabwe suspended raw mineral exports, sparking concerns over supply disruptions, at a time when growing energy storage demand is projected to drive a boom in the market.

image.text: Illustration shows block with the symbol, atomic number and mass number of Lithium (Li) element

data.text: China's lithium price soars after Zimbabwe suspends export

data.snippet: Copper dipped on Thursday as rising inventories and a firmer dollar tempered optimism about a revival in demand from China, the world's top consumer of the metal.

image.text: An electric copper wire is seen in this illustration picture

data.text: Copper eases from two-week high as rising stocks temper demand optimism

data.snippet: CME Group  reopened trading in its Globex metals and natural gas futures and options markets on Wednesday after a brief halt due to technical issues.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Illustration shows CME Group Inc logo

data.text: CME Group reopens Globex metals, natural gas trading after brief technical halt

data.snippet: Global crude steel production in January fell 6.5% from a year earlier to 147.3 million metric tons, World Steel Association data showed on Thursday.

image.text: A labourer marks steel bars at a steel and iron factory in Huai'an

data.text: Global steel output falls 6.5% yr/yr in January -WorldSteel

data.snippet: Australia's BlueScope Steel rejected a A$15 billion ($10.7 billion) revised bid from SGH and U.S.-based Steel Dynamics, sending its shares down almost 5% on Thursday, but said it was open to further talks.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Operations at BlueScope steelworks, Port Kembla

data.text: BlueScope spurns $10.7 billion offer, but leaves door open to talks

data.snippet: Iron ore futures struggled for direction on Thursday, as traders weighed reduced demand for feedstock from imminent production cuts versus signs that Beijing will implement more property stimulus measures.

image.text: Iron ore open pit mine in Kryvyi Rih

data.text: Iron ore flat, traders weigh production cuts against stimulus promises

data.snippet: Eramet will suspend its mineral sands production in Senegal from the end of March following a fire, the French mining group said on Thursday.
data.text: Fire causes Eramet to halt Senegal mineral sands output from end of March

data.snippet: Zimbabwe has suspended exports of all raw minerals and lithium concentrates with immediate effect, its mines ministry said in a statement on Wednesday, after the government alleged malpractices and leakages.

image.text: Workers work at Prospect Lithium Zimbabwe mine in Goromonzi

data.text: Zimbabwe bans exports of all raw minerals and lithium concentrates, cites malpractices

data.snippet: Australia's Lynas Rare Earths reported its best first-half profit in three years on Thursday, helped by higher sales volumes and elevated commodity prices.

image.text: Small toy figure and mineral imitation are seen in front of the Lynas Rare Earths logo in this illustration

data.text: Australia's Lynas posts best interim profit in three years, still misses estimates

data.snippet: Ecuador's National Assembly on Thursday approved reforms proposed by President Daniel Noboa aimed at boosting the nation's mining sector, but that have been criticized by environmentalists and indigenous groups.
data.text: Ecuadorean lawmakers pass reform geared at driving mining investment

data.snippet: Ghana plans to channel about 127 metric tons of gold per year from artisanal and small‑scale mining (ASM) into official trade under revised sector reforms to boost foreign-exchange earnings and stem smuggling losses, the country's finance minister said on Wednesday.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Gold bars at a smelting facility in Accra, Ghana

data.text: Ghana targets 127 tons of artisanal gold annually under sweeping reforms
